steps.title: Identify the shared mechanism
steps.content: The researchers found identical bacterial genera in leaves and roots and offered two mechanisms showing how bacteria can move between plant locations: rain splashing soil onto leaves, and wind transporting bacteria.

steps.title: Determine the logical conclusion
steps.content:
The correct answer directly follows from both mechanisms: if bacteria move between plant parts via the two pathways described, then identical bacteria in leaves and roots must originate from a common source.

Claims about benefit and vulnerability address topics not in the passage, while focusing on wind deposits alone is narrower than what both mechanisms combined suggest.

That is the correct answer.
